questions.question: Why does a parent handler fire twice when a component re-emits a native event without `defineEmits`?
options: Vue has a bug with native event re-emission
The event fires via `$attrs` fallthrough AND via the explicit `$emit()` call
The browser fires the event twice for component elements
Native events always bubble twice in Vue

questions.question: Which of these fixes the double-firing issue when a component has a single root element?
options: Add `.stop` modifier to the parent listener
Remove the explicit `$emit` and let the event fall through automatically

questions.question: What does declaring a native event name in `defineEmits` prevent?
options: The native event from firing at all
The event from reaching child elements
The listener from falling through via `$attrs` to the root element
The event from being passed to scoped slots
